analysis_description: Round 3 tape IV smile diagnostics: Black–Scholes call implied vol from mids (r=0, T=dte_eff/365 with dte_eff from round3work/plotting/original_method/combined_analysis/plot_iv_smile_round3.py — aligns round3description.txt mapping CSV historical day 0→DTE 8 at open, 1→7, 2→6, plus intraday DTE wind-down). On 500 random timestamps per day, compared RMSE of fitted IV vs market IV for quadratic smile fit on closest 3 vs 6 strikes vs flat ATM IV; recorded median top-of-book spread width in ticks for VEV and extract.
used_to_decide: Median IV RMSE vs mids was lower for k=6 than k=3 on days 0–2 (e.g. day0: k6 0.089 vs k3 0.161 vs flat 0.121), supporting fitting the smile on more local strikes for surface accuracy; small sweep trader_v1 (k=3) underperformed trader_v0 (k=6) in backtest total PnL (33466 vs 36834), so v0 kept. Spread median 3 ticks on vouchers/extract — informs MM_EDGE=2 as inside typical spread.

analysis_description: Greek extension of local-smile diagnostics: computed BS implied vols from mids, then fitted local quadratic smile for k=6 and k=3 per sampled timestamp; measured model mispricing in ticks plus fitted vega/delta by strike. T/DTE convention matches round3description + plot_iv_smile_round3 (historical CSV day d -> DTE open 8-d, intraday wind-down, T=dte_eff/365).
used_to_decide: Across days 0–2, k=6 had much tighter p90 abs mispricing than k=3 (~1.53/2.33/2.01 ticks vs ~4.49/4.40/3.75), and vega concentrated around 5100-5400 (median abs vega often ~170-290) while far wings were near zero. Implemented vega-scaled quote size/edge in trader_v2 so central strikes quote larger/tighter and wings smaller/wider while preserving closest-k smile thesis.

analysis_description: Per-product median top-of-book spread (ask1−bid1) on full tape for days 0–2, to relate quote edges to typical liquidity by strike. Deep OTM/ITM (e.g. VEV_4000/4500) show 16–21 tick medians; near-money cluster 5100–5200 are 3–4 ticks. Extract and hydrogel medians 5 and 16 ticks. Same CSV columns as backtester tape.
used_to_decide: Wing strikes are very wide; central strikes match prior ~3-tick context. Swept v3: lower BASE_EDGE_VEV (2) and faster smile refit (50 ticks) on top of v2 vega rules — backtest total PnL improved vs v2 (39307 vs 37230), so v3 is the new best; v2 remains a simpler parent if we need a fallback.

analysis_description: IV level vs |log-moneyness| buckets on Round 3 tapes: bisection IV from mids, r=0, T per dte_eff/365. Median IV rises sharply in the wing bucket (0.10,0.15] and (0.15,1.0] vs near-ATM (0,0.03] and (0.03,0.06] (~0.24 vs ~0.37–0.65), consistent with a steep implied smile at extreme strikes. Output: iv_vs_log_moneyness.json.
used_to_decide: Motivated log-moneyness-based quote widening on wings (v4/v5) in addition to vega, since both spread width and market IV level rise off spot. v4 (two-tier bonus) backtest 39036 < v3 39307; v5 (single +2 if |log m|>0.12) 39237 still < v3. Keep v3 as production line; v5 kept as simpler wing tweak than v4. No pivot off local closest-k surface thesis—only microstructure of edges.

analysis_description: Link between extract level and near-strike implied vol: at each subsampled timestamp, take median IV of the three option mids with smallest |K-S| (bisection IV, r=0, T=dte_eff/365). Spearman correlation between VELVETFRUIT_EXTRACT mid and that near-strike IV: day0 rho≈-0.20, day1≈-0.36, day2≈+0.53 (n=2000 each). Sign and strength vary by historical day, so a simple S→IV fair overlay is not stable; local smile from strip remains the primary handle.
used_to_decide: Grid sweep trader_v6 (closest-k=5, else v3): PnL 37412 < v3 39307. Agrees with prior tape result that k=6 was a better local-fit width than k=3; k=5 is in between and did not beat k=6. No pivot; retain v3 as best. Future work could test REFIT/EMA on v3 only, not a global extract-IV rule from this mixed-sign correlation.

analysis_description: Closest-k grid refresh with local smile pricing residuals: for each sampled timestamp/day and each k in {4,5,6,7}, compute bisection IV from mids, fit quadratic local smile on closest-k strikes, then compare BS-theoretical voucher prices vs mids in ticks (median abs and p90 abs). Time convention remains dte_eff/365 with dte_eff = 8-day - intraday progress from round3description mapping.
used_to_decide: k=6 remained the most robust local-surface width (best or tied-best p90 abs residual on most days), while k=7 over-smoothed and k=4/5 were less stable in tails. Nonetheless, to keep iteration discipline and parent diversity we tested trader_v7 with k=4 on top of v3 microstructure; it underperformed materially (32864 vs 39307), reinforcing k=6 as current best.

analysis_description: Greek: Black–Scholes call theta (r=0, T=dte_eff/365) at tape-implied IV per row; median |theta| by strike over full tape. Time-decay magnitude peaks around 5200-5300 (e.g. day0 median |theta| ~1680-1690) and is much lower on far wings — consistent with prior vega concentration near spot.
used_to_decide: Motivated a refit cadence test: trader_v8 uses REFIT_EVERY=40 vs v3=50, else identical. Backtest total 37603 < v3 39307 — more frequent smile refit did not help. v3 remains best; if retesting refit, use smaller deltas or adjust EMA(S) smoothing instead of only the smile loop.

analysis_description: Family-13 pivot analysis: built calm/neutral/stressed regime from zscore(rv_ann - iv_atm). rv_ann = rolling std of extract log returns (window 60 ticks), annualized by sqrt(10000). iv_atm = median implied vol of 3 strikes closest to spot (bisection IV from mids). Regime thresholds: calm z<=-0.8, stressed z>=0.8, else neutral. Computed occupancy, one-step transition matrix, and proxy PnL-by-regime from next-step extract return.
used_to_decide: Regimes are persistent (calm->calm ~0.712, neutral->neutral ~0.773, stressed->stressed ~0.731) with occupancy roughly neutral 54.3%, calm 23.6%, stressed 22.1%, so a 3-state machine is stable/interpretable. Implemented trader_v9 with regime width multipliers on voucher edges (calm 0.8x, neutral 1.0x, stressed 1.25x) while keeping local-smile/vega logic unchanged. Backtest improved to 39466 vs prior best 39307 (v3).

analysis_description: Duplicate-avoidance adjustment within family-13: switched from plain threshold regime classifier (v9) to a nearby but distinct state machine with hysteresis + minimum state persistence. State uses rv-iv zscore as before, but entry/exit thresholds differ by current state (sticky transitions), and regime affects both width and size multipliers. This preserves interpretability while reducing churn around threshold boundaries.
used_to_decide: Given missing separate parent-index-13 artifacts in this checkout, duplicate risk for plain RV-IV thresholding was treated as high. Implemented trader_v10 as distinct nearby variant: hysteresis regime gating + persistence (same RV-IV signal) and regime-dependent width+size. Backtest 39325 (worse than v9 39466 but close), retaining v9 as best in this branch while satisfying non-duplicate requirement.

analysis_description: Contract-by-contract response/spread-shock study requested in prompt: regress voucher mid changes on extract mid changes (beta_all, beta_up, beta_down), compute lag-1 beta, and measure spread widening after large underlying moves (|dS| >= day p90). This quantifies elasticity/lead-lag/asymmetry and shock spread behavior by strike.
used_to_decide: Observed strong monotonic beta term structure (high for low strikes, near zero for 6000/6500), tiny lag-1 beta (weak lead/lag), and mild up/down asymmetry (generally small, larger at deep ITM). Shock spread widening is modest and strike-specific. Implemented trader_v11 with regime edge multiplier partly scaled by strike beta and a small shock-triggered widen bump. Backtest 39285 < v9 39466, so keep v9 as best while preserving interpretability.

analysis_description: Extract shock vs near-ATM implied vol: p90 of |d extract mid| from pooled diffs, compare mean |dIV_ATM| (median IV of 3 closest strikes) on shock vs calm, and Spearman/corr of |dS| with |dIV_ATM| (atm_iv_dS_lead_lag.json, analyze_atm_iv_shock_lead.py).
used_to_decide: Tape shows |dIV_ATM| is larger after large |dU| and corr(|dS|,|dIV|) is modestly positive (~0.26). trader_v12 adds: if |dU| change since last tick is at or above the tape p90 (2.0 ticks), add +1 tick voucher edge and scale size x0.88 on all strikes (parent v9 regime + local smile + vega sizing). Backtest 39918 (new best vs v9 39466). Documents extract-wide shock re-pricing risk beyond strike-specific betas.

analysis_description: Pooled (days 0–2) |dU| shock vs |dV| by strike: shock = |dU| at pooled p90 (2.0). For each VEV, report mean |dV| on shock vs calm rows, ratio, and Pearson corr at lags -3..3 between dU and dV (voucher_lead_lag_shock.json). Complements OLS betas: contemporaneous lag-0 correlation is very high in 5000–5200 (~0.72–0.75), still ~0.62 at 5300, smaller at 4000/5500; non-zero lags are negligible. Mean |dV| on shocks scales down the chain (e.g. ~1.75 at 4000 vs ~0.12 at 5500) with a roughly uniform 2.2x shock/calm ratio where defined.
used_to_decide: trader_v13 tests a parameter grid on the v12 shock: tier extra edge and size by strike using absolute mean |dV| on shock rows (larger for low strikes, tiny for 5500+). Backtest 39906, below v12 39918 — overshooting widen on 4000/4500 and extra cuts likely hurt. Best line remains v12; tiered absolutes are not a clear win over a flat +1 / x0.88 when combined with vega+regime.

analysis_description: Same voucher_lead_lag_shock.json evidence; grid point two: keep v12 shock magnitudes but apply only to strikes 5000–5300 (highest same-tick corr(dU,dV)); leave deep ITM/OTM on regime+vega-only quoting during shocks.
used_to_decide: trader_v14: shock overlay only on 5000–5300. Backtest 39915, still below v12 39918. Suggests the flat extract-wide v12 adjustment (including wings) captures useful protection or fill mix; gating the overlay to the high-corr core alone is not an improvement. Retain v12 as best; deep-wing behavior left for a future test (e.g. optional wing-only no-shock) if combined with new tape stats.

analysis_description: New contract-level shock propagation with IV/Greek thread (shock_greek_weighted.json): per day and strike compute |dV| shock/calm for |dU|>=p90 and a Greek-weighted shock energy score mean|dV|_shock*(1+median_vega/300), where median_vega is from BS tape-IV inversion every 25 ticks. Energy is highest at 5100, then 5000/5200/4000, and decays sharply to 5400/5500; shock/calm ratio remains ~2.2x broadly.
used_to_decide: Implemented v15 as a targeted shock overlay instead of global v12: apply +1 edge only on high-energy strikes (5000/5100/5200) plus near-adjacent 4500/5300, and soften size reductions (core x0.92, near x0.95) to avoid over-throttling flow. This improved to 39945 (new best, +27 vs v12). Companion grid point v16 (core-only 5000-5300, x0.90) scored 39915, indicating 4500 inclusion and gentler cuts helped.

analysis_description: Parameter-sweep companion to iteration 15 from the same shock_greek_weighted evidence: stricter core-only gating (5000-5300) and uniform core size x0.90.
used_to_decide: v16 underperformed v15 and matched v14-like behavior (~39915), reinforcing that a slightly broader selective overlay with milder size suppression is better than very narrow core-only gating under worse fills.

analysis_description: Shock-row microstructure (shock_spread_asymmetry.json, analyze_shock_spread_asymmetry.py): shock = |d extract| >= day p90. Mean top-of-book spread shock minus calm is near zero (small negative/positive by strike; not a reliable widen signal). On shock rows only, OLS dV~dU through origin split by dU sign: at 5100, median beta_down (~0.535) slightly exceeds beta_up (~0.520) across days — the clearest directional gap in 4500-5300; at 5000/5200/5300 up and down betas are nearly equal. Complements shock_greek_weighted (5100 highest energy).
used_to_decide: trader_v17 = v15 + one extra tick on 5100 only when extract shock and dU<0 (down-shock bucket has higher |dV| per |dU| at 5100 in tape). Backtest 39876 < v15 39945; directional nudge hurt — keep v15 as best, do not ship down-only extra width on 5100 without more evidence.

analysis_description: Grid companion: same shock_greek_weighted / lead-lag band (5000-5300 tight co-movement); test removing 4500 from v15 near shock set so only 5300 remains alongside core 5000/5100/5200.
used_to_decide: trader_v18 (near = {5300} only) total PnL 39945, identical to v15 [14070,17394,8481] — 4500 shock overlay was inert; best unchanged at v15.
